userConfirmedRequirements: Carry page/template categories over to the fresh WordPress site.
Each approved page category needs a distinct Gutenberg template.
Header and footer remain consistent across the site.
Child Counseling should be renamed to Child Therapy in visible/core-service labeling.
Use planning artifacts only until the user approves WordPress surface changes.
The Services menu should be nested with core service pages owning one or more specialty pages where applicable.
Header, footer, and breadcrumbs must mirror LMHG guidance while being implemented in a 100% Gutenberg-native way.
guardrails: Do not import Astro code, Starwind code, legacy CSS, theme templates, block markup, or old plugin implementation artifacts.
Use LMHG plugin data as reference planning material only until the user marks each field/template clean or dirty.
Register custom page templates in theme.json when creating the block theme.
Keep templates in templates/*.html and shared template parts in flat parts/*.html.
Do not put PHP inside block-template HTML files.
Use core Gutenberg blocks before custom blocks or code.

sharedTemplateParts.title: Consistent Site Header
sharedTemplateParts.notes: Carry forward header intent as a fresh Gutenberg template part. Do not import Astro code or old block markup.
sharedTemplateParts.navigationPlan: Use sitewide-navigation-plan header.topLevelItems and the approved services nested menu.

sharedTemplateParts.title: Consistent Site Footer
sharedTemplateParts.notes: Carry forward footer intent as a fresh Gutenberg template part. Do not import Astro code or old block markup.
sharedTemplateParts.navigationPlan: Use sitewide-navigation-plan footer columns and CTA.

templateDefinitions.implementationGate: required when fresh block theme is created

templateDefinitions.title: Home Page Template
templateDefinitions.implementationGate: homepage title and design transplant must be approved before WordPress write

templateDefinitions.title: Services Hub Template
templateDefinitions.implementationGate: separate from home and service detail templates; approved by user as carried forward

templateDefinitions.title: Core Service Page Template
templateDefinitions.implementationGate: core service page family approved as correct; per-page copy/title review still required

templateDefinitions.title: Specialties Hub Template
templateDefinitions.implementationGate: hub layout must be reviewed before WordPress write

templateDefinitions.title: Specialty Page Template
templateDefinitions.implementationGate: design transplant must be reviewed one subtype at a time

templateDefinitions.title: Location and Access Template
templateDefinitions.implementationGate: avoid doorway-page patterns; local claims require review before publishing

templateDefinitions.title: Article Hub Template
templateDefinitions.implementationGate: requires decision whether articles remain pages or move to posts

templateDefinitions.title: Article Detail Template
templateDefinitions.wordpressTemplateType: custom-page-template now; single.html if articles become posts
templateDefinitions.implementationGate: requires decision whether article routes stay pages or become posts

templateDefinitions.title: FAQ Hub Template
templateDefinitions.implementationGate: FAQ schema only after visible FAQ content is confirmed

templateDefinitions.title: FAQ Support Template
templateDefinitions.implementationGate: FAQ schema only after visible FAQ content is confirmed

templateDefinitions.title: Trust and Support Template
templateDefinitions.implementationGate: contact/team/careers/reviews may need pattern variants after user review

templateDefinitions.title: Legal and Utility Template
templateDefinitions.implementationGate: plain content import only after user approves source text and legal/compliance handling

templateDefinitions.title: 404 Template
templateDefinitions.implementationGate: planned; old /not-found/ page content should not be treated as canonical until reviewed

templateDefinitions.title: Hold Template for Unclassified Pages
templateDefinitions.implementationGate: do not implement sample/unclassified page unless user approves keeping it
